description_long: This object is a vintage gas station computer terminal, featuring a tall, rectangular cabinet with a predominantly red and black color scheme. The front panel is equipped with two large reel-to-reel tape drives, a CRT monitor displaying text, and a mechanical keyboard, all set into a metallic frame. A matching chair with a simple, angular design and a cushioned seat is positioned in front of the terminal, suggesting a workstation setup. The side panels are solid, with the left side showing a dark, unadorned surface and the right side revealing the keyboard and part of the chair. The top of the cabinet is flat and beige, while the base is black and slightly recessed. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, evoking mid-20th-century industrial equipment.
description: A vintage gas station computer terminal with a red and black cabinet, reel-to-reel tape drives, CRT monitor, and a matching chair.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red cabinet, angled keyboard, and the chair positioned in front.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying a plain, dark panel with minimal features and the back of the chair.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the red cabinet, keyboard, and the back of the chair.
description_view_3: Front view revealing the tape drives, CRT monitor, keyboard, and the full chair setup.
